active_answer: The Seductive Art of Japanese Bondage
active_question: Which book by Midori is mentioned as an early English reference?
edited_answer: Basic safety and knots can be learned in hours, but developing kinbaku as an emotional, relational practice requires patient, repeated learning throughout life.
edited_question: What does MaestroBD say beginners can learn quickly, and what takes a lifetime?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement trades a bare book-title lookup for the interview's directly useful distinction between quickly learned technical basics and lifelong relational and expressive development.
reason: The replacement trades a bare book-title lookup for the interview's directly useful distinction between quickly learned technical basics and lifelong relational and expressive development.
support_evidence:
Be patient. To learn ropes in the technical way little notion are needed, above all safety, and basic knots, all can be learned in few hours. On the other hand to learn Kinbaku a lifetime is not enough. You never finish learning. So do not rush and practice, learn, practice and learn again and again, and then practice again.
The are courses, masters, books, tutorials and endless ways to learn the basics. Obviously I advice to search for someone to learn from, but that is because I did not have a master when I started. It does not matter how you start, the spirit and the willingness are what really count: ropes are easy to handle and first knots are enough to enjoy, but make them part of what we are and really build emotions it is never-ending and unique journey.
